summary: 18th-century English novelist and dramatist. Author of Tom Jones, Joseph Andrews, Shamela (parody of Richardson's Pamela), Amelia, and the play Tom Thumb. Founded the Bow Street Runners. Sister Sarah wrote The Governess.

works.name: Tom Jones
works.description: Picaresque novel about a foundling raised by Squire Allworthy who marries Sophia Western.

clues.clue: Foundling raised by Squire Allworthy; marries Sophia Western
examples: the title character, who is raised by Squire Allworthy
allowed by Squire Allworthy to marry Sophia Western

clues.clue: Impregnates Black George's daughter Molly; rival Master Blifil
examples: Competition over an inheritance causes acrimony between Bilfil

clues.clue: Sleeps with Mrs. Waters at Upton inn; fears incest (she is Jenny Jones)
examples: rescues and sleeps with Mrs. Waters, only to find out that she is his ostensible mother

clues.clue: Lady Bellaston and Lord Fellamar plot against Sophia
examples: a plot concocted by Lord Fellamar and Lady Bellaston

clues.clue: Mr. Partridge criticizes David Garrick's acting as too realistic
examples: This teacher and barber-surgeon... criticizes David Garrick's acting

works.name: Shamela and Joseph Andrews
works.description: Shamela parodies Richardson's Pamela. Joseph Andrews features Parson Adams.

clues.clue: Shamela — parody of Richardson's Pamela; Lady Booby pursues Squire Booby
examples: published a burlesque 'apology for the life of' a former prostitute

clues.clue: Joseph Andrews — Parson Adams (Aeschylus fan); Joseph recognized as Mr. Wilson's son by strawberry birthmark
examples: Mr. Wilson identifies a strawberry birthmark

clues.clue: Joseph resists Lady Booby; marries Fanny Goodwill
examples: the title character resists the sexual advances of Lady Booby
married by Parson Adams to Fanny Goodwill

works.name: General / Theater
works.description: Plays, Bow Street Runners, Paper War.

clues.clue: Tom Thumb / Tragedy of Tragedies — tiny character swallowed by a red cow
examples: title character defeated a rebel army... then swallowed by a large red cow
founded a London police force called the Bow Street Runners
started The Covent-Garden Journal and declared his opposition to 'hack writers'

clues.clue: Licensing Act of 1737 drove him from theater to novels
examples: retiring from the theater entirely in the wake of the censorship brought about by the Licensing Act

clues.clue: Sister Sarah wrote The Governess (first English children's novel) and David Simple
examples: first full-length English-language children's novel, The Governess
Sarah wrote The Adventures of David Simple

comprehensive_summary: Henry Fielding is an 18th-century English novelist. Tom Jones is about a foundling raised by Squire Allworthy who marries Sophia Western. Shamela parodies Richardson's Pamela. Joseph Andrews features Parson Adams and Joseph's strawberry birthmark. He wrote plays including Tom Thumb before the Licensing Act. He founded the Bow Street Runners. His sister Sarah wrote The Governess.

cards.front: Novel: foundling raised by Squire Allworthy
Novel: sleeps with Mrs. Waters and fears incest
Novel: parody of Richardson's Pamela; Squire Booby and Lady Booby
Novel: title character recognized as Mr. Wilson's son by a strawberry birthmark
Author: founded the Bow Street Runners; Licensing Act of 1737 drove this author from theater to novels
